info.name: A2A OpenCode Wrapper
info.description:
Postman collection for the A2A OpenCode Wrapper APIs.

Covers all endpoints: A2A protocol (Agent Card, JSON-RPC, REST), Context API, and System endpoints.

Use the `baseUrl` variable to point to your running agent:
- Example Agent: http://localhost:3000

variable.description: Base URL of your running A2A agent

request.description: Returns the health status and agent name.

item.name: A2A Protocol
item.description: Agent-to-Agent protocol endpoints (Agent Card, JSON-RPC, REST)

request.description:
Returns the A2A Agent Card with identity, capabilities, and skills.
The `url` field is dynamically rewritten based on the Host header.

Also available at:
- /.well-known/agent.json (legacy)
- /.well-known/agent-json (legacy)

response.name: Agent Card

request.description: Legacy agent card path for older A2A Inspector versions.

Send a task to the agent via JSON-RPC. The agent processes the message using OpenCode + MCP tools and returns the result.

This is a synchronous call — it waits for the full response.

response.name: Task Completed

request.description:
Send a task with SSE streaming. Returns a text/event-stream with real-time status updates, tool execution progress, and the final artifact.

Note: Postman's SSE support is limited — use A2A Inspector or curl for best streaming experience:

curl -N -X POST {{baseUrl}}/a2a/jsonrpc \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{...}'
Retrieve the current state of a previously submitted task by its ID.
Cancel a running task. The agent will abort the OpenCode session and return a canceled status.

Send a follow-up message in the same conversation context.

Using the same `contextId` reuses the OpenCode session, preserving conversation history. This enables multi-turn conversations.

item.name: Context API
item.description: Domain context management — build and retrieve pre-discovered domain knowledge

request.description:
Returns the pre-built domain context file (context.md) as Markdown.

This file contains domain knowledge discovered by the agent using MCP tools.

Returns 404 if the context file hasn't been built yet.

response.name: Context Found
response.body:
# Domain Context

## Workspace Overview
- src/: TypeScript source files
- agents/: Agent configuration files
...

response.name: Context Not Found

request.description:
Trigger domain context discovery using the agent's default contextPrompt from config.

The agent creates a dedicated OpenCode session, uses MCP tools to discover domain data, and writes findings to context.md in the workspace.

⚠️ This is a long-running request (may take 1-5 minutes). Increase Postman's timeout settings.

response.name: Build Completed

request.description:
Trigger domain context discovery with a custom prompt.

Overrides the default contextPrompt from config. Useful for targeted discovery or refreshing specific sections of the context.
